Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Or Tax Defendant Ford Motor Company¿S Memorandum Of Costs
Off Calendar Jun-30-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
James P. Nevin, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Vincent Barnes and Barbara Barnes
Paul Lannus, Esq. of LANKFORD & CRAWFORD LLP (925-300-3520), appearing for defendant / opposing party Ford Motor Company

RULING:
Plaintiffs' motion to strike or tax costs. Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion is granted in part and denied in part. All but $11,200 of defendant Ford Motor Company's claimed costs for witness fees is stricken. Ford's statutory settlement offer was reasonable given the legal and factual posture of the case. Ford failed to establish that any portion of the fees charged by ChemRisk, Inc. were reasonably incurred except for the '[l]ess than 12' hours spent by Dr. Paustenbach as testified to in his deposition. 11 hours multiplied by the reasonably hourly rate of $425 yields a recoverable amount of $4675. Ford failed to establish that any portion of the fees charged by Exponent were reasonably incurred except for the approximately 15 hours spent by Dr. Roberts as testified to in his deposition. 15 hours multiplied by the reasonable hourly rate of $435 yields $6525. $4675 plus $6525 equals $11,200. Ford is awarded the full amount of its claimed costs of $2,717.52 for photocopying of exhibits. While there is a conflict in appellate authority whether cost for copying of exhibits that are not actually used at trial are recoverable, the better reasoned decisions hold that such costs are recoverable where, as here, they were reasonably incurred for trial . (E.g. Benach v. County of Los Angeles (2007) 149 Cal. App. 4th 836, 855-57; Applegate v. St. Francis Lutheran Church (1994) 23 Cal. App. 4th 361, 364). The foregoing is in exercise of the court's discretion under CCP 998(c)(1) and 1033.5(c)(4). Order and judgment signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
